question: How does custody work at Al-Fardan Q9?
answer: Each client account has a dedicated Fireblocks MPC vault. When you open a custody position you get a unique deposit address per asset (BTC / ETH / SOL). Send your crypto to that address — our system auto-detects the on-chain transfer and credits your account after 3 network confirmations. Withdrawals require 2FA + manual admin approval to prevent mistakes. Assets are held 1:1, segregated per client, never re-hypothecated.
aliases: can you tell me about custody
i want to learn about custody
what does al fardan offer for custody

question: Can I withdraw from custody anytime?
answer: Yes. There is no lock-up on custody balances. Submit a withdrawal from the Wallets page — provide the destination address + amount — and our admin team will review + broadcast the Fireblocks transaction. Typical processing time is under 1 hour during UAE business hours.

question: How does staking work at Al-Fardan Q9?
answer: You delegate your ETH or SOL (via your custody vault) to one of our validator partners. Rewards accrue automatically — paid in-kind on the native chain. No lock-up: you can unstake and withdraw with a standard network unbonding period (Ethereum ~27 hours, Solana ~2 days). APY is institutional-grade, currently around 4-7% depending on asset and network conditions. Bitcoin does not support staking.
aliases: can you tell me about staking

question: What's the minimum amount to stake?
answer: Minimum is 1 ETH for Ethereum staking and 1 SOL for Solana. There's no maximum. All rewards are paid in the same asset you staked — no lock-up, compound freely by re-staking.

question: How are staking rewards paid?
answer: Rewards accrue on-chain at the validator level and are paid in-kind (ETH rewards for ETH stakes, SOL rewards for SOL stakes). Ethereum rewards compound automatically into your staked principal. Solana rewards are credited per epoch (~2 days). Your dashboard shows both the accumulated rewards and the USD equivalent at live spot price.

question: How does the OTC Desk work?
answer: OTC is for trades where you don't want market slippage — usually USD 100K+ notional. Submit a quote request (buy or sell, asset, amount) from the OTC page; our dealer provides an indicative quote. You accept via voice confirmation, then we move testnet or mainnet assets between Fireblocks vaults and settle the fiat leg to your registered IBAN. T+0 on crypto, T+1 on fiat.

question: What's the minimum ticket size for OTC?
answer: USD 100,000 notional minimum. This is an institutional service — for smaller orders use a retail exchange. There's no maximum; we have executed single tickets over USD 50M. Spread is typically 10-20 basis points (0.10-0.20%) depending on asset and market conditions.

question: How does lending work at Al-Fardan Q9?
answer: You pledge BTC or ETH as collateral in a Fireblocks multi-sig escrow. We issue a Shariah-compliant Murabaha credit line (USD / AED / EUR) against it at a loan-to-value ratio of your choice up to 75%. Funds are wired to your bank in 24-48 hours. Profit rates start at 3.25% APR for 5-year terms, 4.35% for 1-year. When you repay the principal plus profit, we release the collateral.

question: What is Loan-to-Value (LTV)?
answer: LTV is the percentage of your collateral's USD value that we lend against. A 50% LTV on 1 BTC (at $75K) gives you $37,500 of credit. Lower LTV = lower liquidation risk (price has to fall further before the position triggers). We cap LTV at 60% to give you a 40% buffer against market volatility. The liquidation trigger kicks in when LTV reaches roughly 85% due to a price drop.

question: What happens if my collateral drops in value?
answer: If the market price of your pledged asset falls, your LTV rises. When it crosses roughly 80%, you'll receive a margin call — a 24-hour notice to top up collateral or partially repay the loan. If it reaches 85% before you act, a portion of your collateral is liquidated to bring LTV back to 60%. We use institutional OTC liquidation (no panic-selling on retail exchanges) to minimise slippage.

question: Can I repay the loan early?
answer: Yes — Murabaha structure allows early repayment. Pay the outstanding principal plus cost-to-date and we release the collateral back to your custody vault within 24 hours. There's no early-termination penalty, but the cost already accrued up to the repayment date is non-refundable.

question: Which currencies can I receive the loan in?
answer: USD, AED, or EUR. Funds are wired via SWIFT to your registered IBAN. Conversion to AED uses a transparent fixed rate of 3.67 per USD; EUR uses a live spot rate published by our admin daily. All conversion rates are admin-editable and visible in your calculator before you sign.

question: What is the minimum custody deposit per asset?
answer: Per the Custody Services Agreement (v2.0): Bitcoin minimum 0.001 BTC; Ethereum minimum 0.01 ETH; Solana minimum 0.1 SOL; USDC minimum 50 USDC; Tether (USDT) minimum 50 USDT. There is no maximum on any asset. Eligible deposit assets are BTC, ETH, SOL, USDC, USDT — additional networks added by written notice. Confirmations required: BTC 3 (30-60 min), ETH 12 (2-5 min), SOL 32 (10-15 sec), USDC/USDT ERC-20 12 (2-5 min).

question: Which assets can I deposit into custody?
answer: Five assets currently supported: Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), USD Coin (USDC, ERC-20), and Tether (USDT, ERC-20). Additional networks are added by written notice; tell your relationship manager which asset you'd like supported. If you send an unsupported asset or use an unsupported network, we may either return it (less network fees) or attempt recovery for a fee of $500 or 10% of the asset value, whichever is higher.

question: Are my custody assets segregated and insured?
answer: Yes on both. Assets are held in distinct Fireblocks MPC vaults, separate from the Custodian's treasury — your ownership is tracked on our internal ledger and shows up only on your balance sheet, never ours. Insurance: Lloyd's of London policy SY-2025-49881 with a $250M aggregate annual limit (Apollo, Arch, Ark, Ascot, Aspen syndicates). Policy covers third-party hacks of Fireblocks, theft from custody, insider collusion, loss of private keys, and physical HSM destruction. Market price fluctuations and user error (wrong address / wrong network) are NOT covered.
